message:
refactor(web): Centralize content reporting with in-memory store

New Report Store Module (src/lib/reporting/report-store.ts):
- Create centralized in-memory store for content reports using globalThis
- Define type-safe report schemas:
  - ReportStatus: pending | reviewed | actioned | dismissed
  - ReportAction: dismiss | warn | remove | ban
  - ReportReason: spam | offensive | copyright | harmful | other
  - ReportContentType: prompt | bundle | workflow | collection
- Export REPORT_REASONS array with value/label pairs for UI dropdowns
- Implement type guard functions: isReportReason(), isReportContentType()
- Add getReportReasonLabel() for human-readable reason display

Store Operations:
- createContentReport(): Create new report with auto-generated UUID
- getContentReport(): Retrieve single report by ID
- listContentReports(): Filter/paginate reports by status, contentType, reason
- updateContentReport(): Process report with action and reviewer notes
- getReportStats(): Aggregate counts by status for dashboard
- hasRecentReport(): Detect duplicate reports within time window

API Route Updates (src/app/api/reports/route.ts):
- Replace inline REPORT_REASONS/CONTENT_TYPES with store imports
- Use type guards for validation instead of Set.has()
- Add content title length validation (MAX_TITLE_LENGTH: 140)
- Use createContentReport() for persistence
- Use hasRecentReport() for duplicate detection

Admin API Updates (src/app/api/admin/reports/route.ts):
- Remove mock data array and use listContentReports()
- Use getReportReasonLabel() for human-readable labels
- Use getReportStats() for dashboard statistics
- Use updateContentReport() for moderation actions
- Maintain full audit trail with reviewer ID and notes

Admin Moderation Page (src/app/admin/moderation/page.tsx):
- Remove embedded mock data
- Import store functions for report listing and stats
- Use getReportReasonLabel() for display formatting

This refactoring establishes a foundation for persistent storage migration
while providing immediate functionality for development and testing.
The in-memory store can be replaced with database calls without API changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
